question: [File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/config/RestJavaConfiguration.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: readInternal()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the converter calls message.toBuilder().mergeFrom(inputMessage.getBody(), extensionRegistry).build() where the builder is derived from the already-parsed message, and supply a body that encodes only the sigMap field with attacker-controlled entries, causing the merge to append fake signer approvals to the existing sigMap?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/config/RestJavaConfiguration.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: initialize()] Can an unprivileged external user send a transaction type enum value in a query parameter using Unicode lookalike characters that the case-insensitive ApplicationConversionService converter normalizes to a valid enum value, bypassing a transaction-type-based authorization check that only validates exact ASCII enum names?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/config/RestJavaConfiguration.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: readInternal()] Can an unprivileged external user send a protobuf body with a varint-encoded field length that causes the protobuf parser to read beyond the intended message boundary during mergeFrom(), consuming bytes from a subsequent request in a keep-alive connection and injecting those bytes as part of the current transaction's authorization fields?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/config/RestJavaConfiguration.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: protobufHttpMessageConverter()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the MediaType('application', 'protobuf') construction (which lacks a subtype wildcard check) combined with isCompatibleWith() to send a content type like 'application/protobuf+json' that passes compatibility, triggering the mergeFrom path on a JSON-encoded body that the protobuf parser misinterprets as binary field data, injecting garbage into the transaction's signer fields?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/config/RestJavaConfiguration.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: readInternal()] Can an unprivileged external user send a transaction where the bodyBytes field encodes a CryptoApproveAllowance transaction body with an attacker-chosen owner and spender, and the mergeFrom call does not re-validate that the owner field matches the transaction's actual signer, allowing the fee estimation to treat the allowance as owner-approved?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/config/RestJavaConfiguration.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: readInternal()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the double-read in readInternal() to send a body where the first read produces a Transaction with signedTransactionBytes set, and the mergeFrom injects a bodyBytes field, causing the downstream TransactionFeeContext to prefer bodyBytes over signedTransactionBytes and process an unsigned transaction body as if it were owner-approved?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/config/RestJavaConfiguration.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: etagFilter()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the ShallowEtagHeaderFilter's ETag computation (based on response body hash) to predict the ETag of a future transaction authorization response by pre-computing the hash of the expected response body, then use that ETag in an If-None-Match header to suppress the actual authorization check and receive a 304 response implying prior approval?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/config/RestJavaConfiguration.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: readInternal()] Can an unprivileged external user send a protobuf Transaction where the sigMap contains sigPair entries with empty pubKeyPrefix values, which the mergeFrom call appends to the existing sigMap, causing the numTxnSignatures count to be inflated and the system to believe more owners have approved the transaction than actually have?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/config/RestJavaConfiguration.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: protobufHttpMessageConverter()] Can an unprivileged external user send a protobuf body with a field encoded using wire type 2 (length-delimited) for a field that the schema defines as wire type 0 (varint), which the empty ExtensionRegistry treats as an unknown field, but which a downstream component interprets as a valid authorization token, bypassing the signer check?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/config/RestJavaConfiguration.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: readInternal()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the IOException declared in the readInternal() override to send a body that causes an IOException mid-stream during mergeFrom(), resulting in a partially-merged Message being built and returned with incomplete signer validation data, allowing a transaction to be processed without full owner approval?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/config/RestJavaConfiguration.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: readInternal()] Can an unprivileged external user send a protobuf body that encodes a Transaction with a signedTransactionBytes field containing a SignedTransaction where the bodyBytes encodes a CryptoDeleteAllowance transaction, and the mergeFrom call injects a different transaction type into the body, causing the authorization check to be performed against the wrong transaction type?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/config/RestJavaConfiguration.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: protobufHttpMessageConverter()] Can an unprivileged external user send a request with Content-Type 'application/protobuf' and a body that is a valid protobuf encoding of a Transaction message but with the sigMap field encoded at a field number that the current schema version does not recognize, causing the empty ExtensionRegistry to store it as an unknown field and the downstream parser to see an empty sigMap, treating the transaction as requiring no signer approval?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/config/RestJavaConfiguration.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: etagFilter()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the ShallowEtagHeaderFilter's buffering of the entire response body on /api/* to cause a memory exhaustion condition that forces the server to return a cached (stale) authorization response for a different transaction, bypassing the requirement for fresh signer validation?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/config/RestJavaConfiguration.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: readInternal()] Can an unprivileged external user send a protobuf body where the Transaction message's signedTransactionBytes field is set to a valid SignedTransaction, and the mergeFrom call injects a second signedTransactionBytes value that overwrites the first (last-write-wins for singular bytes fields), replacing the legitimate signer's signed bytes with attacker-controlled bytes that claim a different owner?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/config/RestJavaConfiguration.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: readInternal()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that inputMessage.getHeaders().getContentType() is called after super.readInternal() has already consumed the body, and if the Content-Type header is modified between the two reads (e.g., via a proxy), the isCompatibleWith check could produce a different result than expected, causing the mergeFrom path to be taken or skipped unexpectedly, altering the transaction's signer data?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/config/RestJavaConfiguration.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: protobufHttpMessageConverter()] Can an unprivileged external user send a protobuf Transaction body where the sigMap field is encoded twice in the binary payload (duplicate field tags), causing the protobuf parser to merge both encodings and produce a sigMap with entries from both, allowing the attacker to inject fake signer entries alongside a legitimate signer's entry?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/config/RestJavaConfiguration.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: readInternal()] Can an unprivileged external user send a protobuf body that encodes a Transaction with a bodyBytes field (legacy format) containing a TransactionBody with a CryptoApproveAllowance body, and the mergeFrom call injects a signedTransactionBytes field that encodes a different transaction type, causing the downstream parser to process the wrong transaction body for authorization?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/config/RestJavaConfiguration.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: initialize()] Can an unprivileged external user send a FeeEstimateMode query parameter value that the case-insensitive ApplicationConversionService converter maps to the STATE enum value (e.g., 'state', 'State', 'STATE'), causing the fee estimation to use the STATE mode code path which reads live token/topic custom fee data from the database, potentially revealing information about other accounts' authorization states?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/config/RestJavaConfiguration.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: readInternal()] Can an unprivileged external user send a protobuf body where the Transaction message contains both signedTransactionBytes and bodyBytes fields set, and the mergeFrom call appends additional data to both fields, causing the downstream TransactionFeeContext to use the merged bodyBytes (which may contain attacker-injected owner fields) instead of the signed transaction bytes?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/config/RestJavaConfiguration.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: protobufHttpMessageConverter()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the converter's supported media types list is built by appending the custom 'application/protobuf' type to the existing list (new ArrayList<>(converter.getSupportedMediaTypes())), and if the existing list contains a wildcard type, any content type would trigger the mergeFrom path, allowing injection of arbitrary protobuf fields into the transaction?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/config/RestJavaConfiguration.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: readInternal()] Can an unprivileged external user send a protobuf Transaction where the sigMap's sigPair entries contain valid-length but cryptographically invalid signatures, and the mergeFrom call appends additional sigPair entries, causing the numTxnSignatures count to be inflated while none of the signatures actually authorize the transaction on behalf of the claimed owner?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/config/RestJavaConfiguration.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: readInternal()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the mergeFrom(inputMessage.getBody(), extensionRegistry) call to inject a protobuf-encoded TransactionBody with a nodeAccountID field set to an attacker-chosen node, causing the fee estimation to use a different node's fee schedule that does not require the same level of signer authorization?
